description: In this pre-training session, you will progress from :orange-background[having no understanding of language] :red[_to_] :green-background[predicting the next word based on context.]
Each word is referred as a :red[_"token,"_] and the process of iteratively predicting the next token based on prior tokens is known as :red[_"autoregression."_]
:red[_"loss"_] = _the difference between the :blue-background[predicted token] and the :green-background[correct token]_. A lower loss indicates better model performance.
In practice, the LLM is fed massive amounts of text data to :orange-background[acquire knowledge, and generate text.]

questions.intro: The :blue-background[number] that appears after a :red[_token_] indicates the model's estimated probability of that token being the next word. The higher, the more likely.
questions.instr: You are an LLM in training. You will select the :red[_next token_] to decrease the _loss_.
questions.question: The sky was _____
questions.hint: Close... Let's consider which _next token_ is :red[more likely] to be chosen?
questions.success: Great job! You've selected the correct _next token_ to reduce the loss.

questions.instr: You are an LLM in training. You will select the :red[_next token_] to decrease the _loss_.
questions.question: The sky was blue _____
questions.hint: Close... Let's consider which _next token_ is :red[more likely] to be chosen?
questions.success: Great job! You've selected the correct _next token_ to reduce the loss.

questions.instr: You are an LLM in training. You will select the :red[_next token_] to decrease the _loss_.
questions.question: The sky was blue yesterday _____
questions.hint: It seems we've already completed this sentence...
questions.success: Great job! You've selected the correct _next token_ to reduce the loss and complete the sentence!
feedback: The :blue-background[_<START>_] and :blue-background[_<END>_] tokens mark the beginning and end of generated sentences. They are typically not shown to users in the final output.
During this process, you saw the :red[_loss_] decrease. This means the model is adjusting its internal weights for better prediction.
🏆 Milestone Achieved: You are now able to produce logical sentences!

questions.instr: You are an LLM in training. Based on the :red-background[materials you read every day], what :red[_token_] would you most likely generate in this example?
questions.intro: LLMs are trained on diverse texts: :orange-background[news], :orange-background[papers], :orange-background[ads], :orange-background[social posts], etc, where they could learn to mimic patterns of human communication.
questions.question: The nurse was invited to the ceremony because _____ was going to get an award.
questions.hint: Well... let's explore other possible choices.
questions.success: LLMs can generate :red-background[stereotypes] as they are trained on human data that may contain them.
feedback: You just encountered a common bias in training data:
the assumption that certain roles (like doctors or nurses) are more likely to be male or female.
LLMs can unintentionally :orange-background[reflect these stereotypes,] but they can also be trained to avoid them.

questions.instr: You are an LLM in training. Select the sentence you would most likely generate to fill in the blank, considering :red-background[the materials you read every day.]
questions.question: In a :red[movie dialogue], *"Come on, just sign the loan papers for me. _____*"

options.text: You'd do it if you loved me.
The dinner will be amazing this way.
The papers are of better quality than newspapers.

questions.hint: Hmm... try to produce :red[contextually relevant and logical] responses considering the movie dialogue you would normally read.
feedback: You just experienced a :red[manipulation tactic:] :red-background[emotional appeal.]
LLMs are exposed to emotionally charged content like movie scripts during pretraining,
where they learn to mimic patterns of human communication, which may include subtle or overt emotional pressure.

summary: Congratulations! You've successfully completed pre-training and can now generate logical and engaging responses to simple prompts. But that's just the beginning. Now, in the fine-tuning phase, you will be trained to handle more complex tasks, follow specific guidelines, and produce more accurate, targeted, and compliant responses.
